items.name: sharpened rebar
items.desc: Cut from the seawall project. The seawall didn't need it, evidently.

items.name: claw hammer
items.desc: Drove a thousand honest nails before the retraining.

items.name: brick in a sock
items.desc: The argyle one. It was laundry day when it mattered.

items.name: tire iron
items.desc: The car it came with is a planter on Nostrand now.

items.name: straight razor
items.desc: Folds away to nothing. So did the barbershop.

items.name: fillet knife
items.desc: Skinned eels off the Sheepshead piers for years. It adapts.

items.name: toothbrush shiv
items.desc: Somebody did real time on this. The workmanship shows.

items.name: hand hatchet
items.desc: Hardware-store grade. The hardware store is a church now, which complicates returns.

items.name: pipe wrench
items.desc: Eighteen inches of plumber's authority. Grips arguments by the threads.

items.name: bike chain
items.desc: Greased, knotted at one end. The bike is missed; the chain moved on.

items.name: meat cleaver
items.desc: From a halal cart that defended its corner twice and lost it once.

items.name: ice pick
items.desc: From when ice was a delivery. Ice is a delivery again.

items.name: board with nails
items.desc: Carpentry's contribution to diplomacy. The nails are reclaimed, like everything.

items.name: brass knuckles
items.desc: Pawned and redeemed six times, like a soul.

items.name: salvaged nine iron
items.desc: From a drowned course in the Rockaways. Fore.

items.name: fire axe
items.desc: IN CASE OF EMERGENCY, said the glass. It has been a long emergency.

items.name: pipe shotgun
items.desc: Two pipes and a nail. Don't stand near either end.

items.name: pocket twenty-two
items.desc: Quiet, polite, fits in a sock. Has settled estates.

items.name: converted nail gun
items.desc: Construction grade, reclassified by the street as finish work.

items.name: pawnshop carbine
items.desc: Sporterized before the war, sawed down after. Its third owner is its proudest.

items.name: flare pistol
items.desc: Marine surplus. Technically a signaling device, and the signal is widely understood.

items.name: phonebook vest
items.desc: Yellow pages duct-taped under a hoodie. Coverage ends at the letter K.

items.name: motorcycle jacket
items.desc: The road rash on the left side belonged to someone who didn't need it back.

items.name: plywood sandwich board
items.desc: REPENT on the front, lunch specials on the back. Stops glancing blows and most conversation.

items.name: decommissioned riot pads
items.desc: Surplus from a department that no longer answers the phone it no longer has.

items.name: freezer suit
items.desc: Meatpacking insulation. Knives find it discouraging. So does August.

items.name: oyster waders
items.desc: Punctures patched with bike rubber. Smells like low tide, permanently, everywhere.

items.name: pigeon skewer
items.desc: Squab, the vendor insists, and zoologically he is correct.

items.name: rooftop honey
items.desc: From the Bed-Stuy hives. Tastes faintly of tar and optimism.

items.name: smoked eel
items.desc: Caught off Hunts Point. The sheen is a smoking artifact, probably.

items.name: city ration brick
items.desc: Relief issue, 2032 print run. Dense enough to double as a weapon, and flavored accordingly.

items.name: day-old knish
items.desc: Day-old is a designation, not a count.

items.name: egg on a roll
items.desc: Salt, pepper, ketchup. Civilization's load-bearing sandwich.

items.name: mystery jerky
items.desc: The vendor says beef. The vendor also says he loves you.

items.name: canned peaches
items.desc: Syrup from before. People eat them slowly, looking at nothing.

items.name: fire-escape tomato
items.desc: Grown in a bucket six floors up and guarded like a reliquary.

items.name: jar of stairwell hooch
items.desc: Distilled in a stairwell with conviction. The blindness is mostly rumor.

items.name: cricket protein bar
items.desc: PROTEIN, the wrapper shouts. The legs confirm.

items.name: chicken over rice
items.desc: White sauce, hot sauce, foil tray. Some things held.

items.name: instant noodle brick
items.desc: Eat it dry like a cracker or wet like soup. Either way, a brick first.

items.name: fish antibiotics
items.desc: Aquarium-grade amoxicillin. You are the aquarium now.

items.name: suture kit
items.desc: Curved needle, waxed thread, instructions drawn from somebody's worse day.

items.name: tube of burn gel
items.desc: Half-used. The first half has a story too.

items.name: antiseptic moonshine
items.desc: Stinging means it's working, says everyone who is not currently being stung.

items.name: ambulance trauma kit
items.desc: Ambulance stock, route unknown. The tourniquet alone is a month's rent.

items.name: botánica poultice
items.desc: The botánica swears by it, and the botánica has outlived two pharmacies.

items.name: splint kit
items.desc: Two slats and a roll of cloth, priced like the X-ray you won't be getting.

items.name: bottle of iodine
items.desc: Paints you amber and your odds slightly better.

items.name: transit master key
items.desc: Opens gates, sheds, signal rooms, and certain careers.

items.name: pre-crash hundred
items.desc: Legal tender, technically. Mostly bought as a souvenir of believing in things.

items.name: brass hydrant cap
items.desc: Eleven pounds of brass. The hydrant is somebody else's problem now.

items.name: tin of heirloom seeds
items.desc: Tomato, squash, bean, hand-labeled. A whole future, four inches square.

items.name: insulin cooler
items.desc: Cold chain intact, says the sticker. Lives downstream believe the sticker.

items.name: warped vinyl record
items.desc: Warped just enough that the love songs sound sincere.

items.name: strand of bridge cable
items.desc: Cut from a footbridge nobody official will admit is missing it.

items.name: stadium seat slat
items.desc: Blue paint, a section number, forty years of other people's hope.

items.name: brass ferry bell
items.desc: Engraved with a boat that sank twice: once in water, once at auction.

items.name: dead cloud drive
items.desc: A terabyte of somebody's everything. No port left in the city fits it.

items.name: church candlesticks
items.desc: Donated, says the engraving. Liberated, says the seller.

items.name: stamped cigarette carton
items.desc: Three states' tax stamps, all forged, all beautiful.

items.name: single pearl earring
items.desc: The other one is in the harbor with the evening it ended.

items.name: lump of ambergris
items.desc: Harbor-found whale gallstone. Smells like money because it is.

items.name: expired farecard
items.desc: Insufficient fare, eternally. People keep them like saints' cards.

items.name: stack of takeout menus
items.desc: For restaurants that are now apartments, fires, or legends.

items.name: skyline snow globe
items.desc: Shake it and the towers are still lit. People do this more than they admit.

items.name: ring of unknown keys
items.desc: Every door they opened is somebody else's now.

items.name: participation trophy
items.desc: EVERYONE'S A WINNER, 2024. A pre-war sentiment in every possible way.

items.name: mannequin hand
items.desc: Waves at strangers from the top of your bag. Worth it.

items.name: losing scratch tickets
items.desc: Forty dollars of optimism, redeemed for zero.

items.name: unlabeled cassette
items.desc: Somebody's mixtape. Side B is a man crying, then apologizing for it.
